items.q: What attitude does Pop Art take toward consumer culture?
items.correct: Ambiguous - it both celebrates and critiques mass media imagery.
items.why: Students read Pop Art as either pure celebration or pure protest. Warhol's deadpan repetition of Marilyn or soup cans is deliberately AMBIGUOUS, leaving viewers to decide if it's homage or critique.

items.correct: An object's design should be determined by its purpose.
items.misconception: Decoration is the most important aspect of design.
items.random_wrong: Designers must follow strict government forms.
items.why: Students invert the meaning. Sullivan's modernist principle prioritises FUNCTION over ornament - the chair's shape should serve sitting, not the other way around. Postmodernism later challenged this.

items.q: What is the first step of formal analysis?
items.correct: Description - noting what is actually present in the work.
items.why: Students jump to meaning immediately. Formal analysis follows a sequence: describe, analyse formal elements, interpret, evaluate. Description grounds interpretation in what's actually there.

items.q: Who created the 'Angel of the North' sculpture installed in 1998?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Antony Gormley. “Anish Kapoor” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Whose 'Infinity Rooms' are famous immersive installations?
items.why: It's Yayoi Kusama. “Olafur Eliasson” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Gormley's 'Angel of the North' installed?
items.why: The real figure is 1998. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
